Must be planted in blocks (minimum 4 rows) not single rows â€” corn is wind-pollinated and isolated plants produce poor, gappy ears. Missouri summers are ideal for sweet corn; harvest when silk turns brown and kernels are milky. Isolation required if growing supersweet varieties near regular sweet corn.
